_comment: Ratchets for the SDK↔OpenAPI contract tests (typescript/test/contract.test.ts and python/tests/test_contract.py). Both SDKs read this one file so coverage is tracked from a single source of truth. The tests stay green today despite known gaps, but FAIL on any NEW drift. Shrink these lists as gaps close; do not grow them to silence newly-introduced drift.

known_uncovered._comment: METHOD path operations the OpenAPI spec declares but the hand-written SDK facade does NOT yet surface a method for. Per language. Adding a method for one of these must be accompanied by removing it here (the 'no now-covered entries' check enforces that). A brand-new spec operation with no method and no entry here fails the suite.

known_undocumented._comment: METHOD path operations the SDK facade calls that are absent from the OpenAPI spec. These are NOT facade bugs — litegen-core serves them, but its OpenAPI emission omits them, so they get no generated types (the TS client hand-writes their types under a 'Types not yet in generated schema' banner). The real fix is to make litegen-core include these in /openapi.json, regenerate, then delete them here. Shared across both SDKs. Anything the facade calls that is neither in the spec nor listed here fails the drift check (catches genuine typos/renames).
